speaker: HELEN
text: If you misdoubt me that I am not she,

speaker: HELEN
text: I know not how I shall assure you further

speaker: HELEN
text: But I shall lose the grounds I work upon.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Though my estate be fall’n, I was well born,

speaker: WIDOW
text: Nothing acquainted with these businesses,

speaker: WIDOW
text: And would not put my reputation now

speaker: WIDOW
text: In any staining act.

speaker: HELEN
text: Nor would I wish you.

speaker: HELEN
text: First give me trust the Count he is my husband,

speaker: HELEN
text: And what to your sworn counsel I have spoken

speaker: HELEN
text: Is so from word to word; and then you cannot,

speaker: HELEN
text: By the good aid that I of you shall borrow,

speaker: HELEN
text: Err in bestowing it.

speaker: WIDOW
text: I should believe you,

speaker: WIDOW
text: For you have showed me that which well approves

speaker: WIDOW
text: You’re great in fortune.

speaker: HELEN
text: Take this purse of gold,

speaker: HELEN
text: And let me buy your friendly help thus far,

speaker: HELEN
text: Which I will overpay and pay again

speaker: HELEN
text: When I have found it. The Count he woos your daughter,

speaker: HELEN
text: Lays down his wanton siege before her beauty,

speaker: HELEN
text: Resolved to carry her. Let her in fine consent

speaker: HELEN
text: As we’ll direct her how ’tis best to bear it.

speaker: HELEN
text: Now his important blood will naught deny

speaker: HELEN
text: That she’ll demand. A ring the County wears

speaker: HELEN
text: That downward hath succeeded in his house

speaker: HELEN
text: From son to son some four or five descents

speaker: HELEN
text: Since the first father wore it. This ring he holds

speaker: HELEN
text: In most rich choice. Yet, in his idle fire,

speaker: HELEN
text: To buy his will it would not seem too dear,

speaker: HELEN
text: Howe’er repented after.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Now I see the bottom of your purpose.

speaker: HELEN
text: You see it lawful, then. It is no more

speaker: HELEN
text: But that your daughter, ere she seems as won,

speaker: HELEN
text: Desires this ring, appoints him an encounter,

speaker: HELEN
text: In fine, delivers me to fill the time,

speaker: HELEN
text: Herself most chastely absent. After,

speaker: HELEN
text: To marry her, I’ll add three thousand crowns

speaker: HELEN
text: To what is passed already.

speaker: WIDOW
text: I have yielded.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Instruct my daughter how she shall persever

speaker: WIDOW
text: That time and place with this deceit so lawful

speaker: WIDOW
text: May prove coherent. Every night he comes

speaker: WIDOW
text: With musics of all sorts and songs composed

speaker: WIDOW
text: To her unworthiness. It nothing steads us

speaker: WIDOW
text: To chide him from our eaves, for he persists

speaker: WIDOW
text: As if his life lay on ’t.

speaker: HELEN
text: Why then tonight

speaker: HELEN
text: Let us assay our plot, which, if it speed,

speaker: HELEN
text: Is wicked meaning in a lawful deed,

speaker: HELEN
text: And lawful meaning in a lawful act,

speaker: HELEN
text: Where both not sin, and yet a sinful fact.

speaker: HELEN
text: But let’s about it.
